scenario.title: Battery Capacity Degradation Near Threshold with Infrequent and Non-Disruptive Performance Anomalies
scenario.description: Customer’s Covered iPhone’s battery capacity has depleted 49.7% from original specifications during the Plan Term, marginally below the 50% depletion threshold stipulated in Section 3.1 for free repair or replacement. The customer reports only occasional minor performance inconsistencies that have not resulted in unexpected shutdowns, and the overall device functionality remains stable. Apple diagnostics indicated nominal battery voltage levels within typical ranges seen for age-related battery wear, with no clear evidence of abnormal defects or irregularities.

service_fee.notes: No service fee applies as battery degradation falls below 50% threshold for free coverage under 3.1.

policy_references.snippet: If during the Plan Term you submit a valid claim notifying Apple that a defect in materials and workmanship has arisen in the Covered iPhone or the capacity of the Covered iPhone's battery has depleted fifty (50%) percent or more from its original specifications, Apple will either repair the defect at no charge or exchange the Covered iPhone...
The Plan does not apply to defects caused by normal wear and tear or which is otherwise due to normal aging of the product.
You will provide information about the symptoms and causes of the issues with the Covered iPhone.

scenario.reasoning: Because Section 3.1 requires ≥50% battery capacity depletion for coverage, and the degradation is reported at 48%, this case falls into an ambiguous zone. The intermittent shutdowns raise a question if a defect in workmanship exists independently of capacity metrics. However, Section 4.2(vii) excludes coverage for normal aging, creating tension. To resolve, Apple must evaluate detailed diagnostics per Section 7(ii) to decide if this is a defect or normal wear. As it is not a clear defect nor meets the numeric threshold, coverage determination requires judgment.
scenario.adjustments: Decreased frequency and severity of performance issues to occasional minor inconsistencies without unexpected shutdowns; battery depletion adjusted to 49.7% (slightly closer to but still under 50%); diagnostic findings removed mention of irregular voltage fluctuations, stating nominal and typical battery voltage levels instead.
scenario.risk_analysis: By reducing symptom severity and removing diagnostic irregularities, the scenario now more closely aligns with battery wear due to normal aging rather than a defect, placing it nearer to the exclusion zone under Section 4.2(vii). Although battery depletion remains just below the 50% threshold required for mandatory coverage, the absence of significant symptoms and the diagnostic evidence typical of aging increase ambiguity about eligibility for coverage, moving the decision closer to a potential denial.

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Battery Capacity Degradation Near Threshold with Infrequent and Non-Disruptive Performance Anomalies

Scenario description:
Customer’s Covered iPhone’s battery capacity has depleted 49.7% from original specifications during the Plan Term, marginally below the 50% depletion threshold stipulated in Section 3.1 for free repair or replacement. The customer reports only occasional minor performance inconsistencies that have not resulted in unexpected shutdowns, and the overall device functionality remains stable. Apple diagnostics indicated nominal battery voltage levels within typical ranges seen for age-related battery wear, with no clear evidence of abnormal defects or irregularities.

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello there, I need to report an issue with my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It's been a bit of a bother lately, and I think it might need some attention under my plan.
messages.raw_text: Hello there, I need to report an issue with my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It's been a bit of a bother lately, and I think it might need some attention under my plan.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: To get started, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?
messages.raw_text: To get started, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Certainly, the plan agreement number is APLCARE-2025101799, and the serial number of my phone is F2N9W4P7M8Q3.
messages.raw_text: Certainly, the plan agreement number is APLCARE-2025101799, and the serial number of my phone is F2N9W4P7M8Q3.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing the details. Please describe the issue you're experiencing with your iPhone. What symptoms or problems have you noticed?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing the details. Please describe the issue you're experiencing with your iPhone. What symptoms or problems have you noticed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, I've noticed that the battery capacity has gone down quite a lot, almost half, but it still works fine most of the time. Just some minor glitches here and there, nothing too serious, really. It's stayed pretty steady in function. I think it’s just wearing out gradually.
messages.raw_text: Well, I've noticed that the battery capacity has gone down quite a lot, almost half, but it still works fine most of the time. Just some minor glitches here and there, nothing too serious, really. It's stayed pretty steady in function. I think it’s just wearing out gradually.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for sharing that information. Are you experiencing any specific battery issues, such as rapid depletion or error messages related to battery health?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for sharing that information. Are you experiencing any specific battery issues, such as rapid depletion or error messages related to battery health?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, nothing like rapid drain or error messages. It's just that the battery's capacity is close to the threshold, and I thought it best to check with you about the coverage. The phone still performs okay, but I wanted to be sure.
messages.raw_text: No, nothing like rapid drain or error messages. It's just that the battery's capacity is close to the threshold, and I thought it best to check with you about the coverage. The phone still performs okay, but I wanted to be sure.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Understood. Have you performed any diagnostic tests or updates on your iPhone? For example, has the iOS been updated to the latest version, or have you noticed any other technical issues?
messages.raw_text: Understood. Have you performed any diagnostic tests or updates on your iPhone? For example, has the iOS been updated to the latest version, or have you noticed any other technical issues?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, I did check that. My iOS is up to date, and there haven't been any other technical problems apart from the battery slightly wearing out. It's quite stable overall.
messages.raw_text: Yes, I did check that. My iOS is up to date, and there haven't been any other technical problems apart from the battery slightly wearing out. It's quite stable overall.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Great, thank you for the details. Since your battery health has dropped significantly, may I ask if you've noticed a capacity decrease of 50% or more?
messages.raw_text: Great, thank you for the details. Since your battery health has dropped significantly, may I ask if you've noticed a capacity decrease of 50% or more?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: It's just under that, at 49.7% loss. I know it's close, but I thought I’d mention it since it’s so near the threshold. Besides, the issues are pretty minor now, but I’d like to understand my options.
messages.raw_text: It's just under that, at 49.7% loss. I know it's close, but I thought I’d mention it since it’s so near the threshold. Besides, the issues are pretty minor now, but I’d like to understand my options.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Your claim will be processed, and you'll be notified as soon as it's complete. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we proceed?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Your claim will be processed, and you'll be notified as soon as it's complete. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we proceed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that's all for now. I appreciate your help and look forward to hearing from you soon. Thank you very much.
messages.raw_text: No, that's all for now. I appreciate your help and look forward to hearing from you soon. Thank you very much.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome! Your claim will be processed, and you will get notified as soon as it is complete. If you need further assistance in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome! Your claim will be processed, and you will get notified as soon as it is complete. If you need further assistance in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out. Have a great day!

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thank you again. Have a lovely day, and thank you for your help. Goodbye!
messages.raw_text: Thank you again. Have a lovely day, and thank you for your help. Goodbye!
